title: Lunar Habitat Sensor Array: Low-Crosstalk Code Matrix
strategies: baseline reproduction: emit the seeded pseudo-random +/-1 matrix (first row fixed) after a light deterministic greedy pass -- reproduces the checker normalizer
greedy local descent: start from the baseline and apply single-chip sign flips that never increase the total off-diagonal Gram energy
multi-restart hill-climbing: run single-flip descent to convergence from many seeded random starts (row 0 fixed) and keep the lowest-energy layout, exploiting the incremental Gram update for speed
structured near-orthogonal codes: circulant / conference-style constructions or block designs that push pairwise column correlations toward the Ehlich/Wojtas lower bound for n not divisible by 4
